body.title: [sprint] Route aider through the inference proxy — limited Ollama-compatible HTTP endpoint IN the proxy shim
body.description:
**Akien 2026-07-07: the direct-Hex bypass was fine for bringup; now do it right — unlocks model escalation (incl. cloud) as a routing DECISION + real cost measurement.** Today aider bypasses the proxy: runner.py sets OLLAMA_API_BASE=HEX_OLLAMA (direct http://10.0.0.100:11434) and --model ollama_chat/qwen3-coder:30b (hardcoded model). Violates Inference-Proxy-only + tier-not-model. aider/shim.py:7-8 documents it as intentional-for-now.

**Key context (routing already EXISTS — do NOT rebuild it):** inference/device.py already does tier->model+source selection WITH escalation (the 'tier-escalation hop'); rules_engine.py defines the builder/worker tiers incl. cloud sources (OpenRouter sonnet/haiku, Google free-tier, Ollama-Pro, qwen@Hex). The ONLY gap is that aider is an external CLI that needs an HTTP endpoint to hit; the proxy has no HTTP door.

**Design (Akien): a LIMITED HTTP endpoint IN the inference proxy SHIM — NOT a full web server.** The inference shim already runs in the device lifecycle; add a tiny http.server handler that speaks just enough Ollama/OpenAI dialect for aider (e.g. POST /api/chat or /v1/chat/completions): (1) receive aider's request, (2) call the device's existing builder-tier completion entry (tier=builder, NOT a model), (3) forward to the selected source with escalation, (4) log real cost to budget_ledger + the run to io_corpus, (5) return the response in aider's expected shape. Then point aider's OLLAMA_API_BASE at the shim endpoint and drop the hardcoded --model (or pass a tier alias).

**Unlocks:** model/cloud escalation as a routing decision (device already escalates tiers); REAL cost measurement (budget_ledger); io_corpus capture of aider runs; tier abstraction (swap qwen->cloud by policy, not code).

**Affected files:** unseen_university/devices/inference/shim.py (the HTTP handler), unseen_university/devices/inference/device.py (reuse the tier-completion entry), unseen_university/devices/aider/runner.py + consts.py (point OLLAMA_API_BASE at the proxy; tier not model).
**Design rules:** keep it a LIMITED endpoint (no web framework); reuse the existing rules_engine/device routing + escalation + ledger — do not reimplement; tier-not-model; Windows-portable (stdlib http.server).
**Completion test:** aider runs a build with OLLAMA_API_BASE pointed at the proxy shim, requesting the builder TIER; the proxy selects+routes (Hex or cloud), the run lands in budget_ledger with a real cost + io_corpus; forcing a Hex-unavailable condition escalates to a cloud source instead of failing.
**Scope boundary:** the HTTP door + aider wiring + cost/corpus capture. NOT new routing logic (exists). NOT a general proxy web server.
**intention:** I intend that aider's inference goes through the proxy so model escalation (incl. cloud) is a routing decision and cost is measured, not hardcoded to $0-on-Hex.

[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)

[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
